Code fragment: public final TableField<AccountRecord, Integer> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is named generically as 'id' rather than a more descriptive name like 'account_id'. Consider renaming the column to 'account_id' to make it self-documenting and avoid ambiguity when joining tables.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the T_ACCOUNT table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., `dsl.select(ACCOUNT.ID, ACCOUNT.NAME).from(ACCOUNT).fetchInto(Account.class)`.
